results.title: Monkey business: primates
results.creator_credit: Jeff Spencer, Chris O'Rourke and Victoria. Education Dept. Curriculum Branch
results.brief_description: Discusses features common to the three main groups of primates. Also provides a behind-the scenes look at the work of the keepers in the primate department of Melbourne zoo.

results.title: Bilingual kids
results.creator_credit: James Mann and Victoria. Dept. of Education and Early Childhood Development
results.brief_description: This program is designed to introduce teachers, parents and communities to the concept of bilingual education. It looks at processes involved in the implementation of the Greek:English Bilingual Pilot Project in the Richmond-Collingwood area. A number of evaluation findings are also discussed

results.title: Issues in education: the Chief Executive - schooling P-12
results.creator_credit: Trevor Lunn and Victoria. Schools Division. Curriculum Branch
results.brief_description: Dr. Graham Allen, Chief Executive, Ministry of Education, explains Victoria’s school curriculum plan for preparatory level to Year 12. The objective is to have the maximum number of students completing Year 12. The curriculum includes a broad education spectrum up to Year 10, with specialized subjects during Years 11-12, enabling astudents to pursue further education or to join the workforce with a knowledge of current technology.
results.creator_credit_for_label: Trevor Lunn and Victoria. Schools Division. Curriculum Branch

results.title: Keyboard skills in a language program
results.creator_credit: Neville Stanley and TV Ed Productions
results.brief_description: Ilustrates a method of teaching keyboard skills to primary school children in an integrated language program. A child centered approach where pupils develop written language to use as keyboard drills instead of using set typing tests. The program describes the use of master work sheets and emphasises ergonomics and computer environments. Keyboard skills are seen as essential computer skills for extensive word processing.

results.title: Making it relevant
results.creator_credit: James Mann and Victoria. Dept. of Education and Early Childhood Development
results.brief_description: Looks at the need to make changes in the curriculum in order to cater for the many students now staying at schools who are perhaps not interested in further education. This program focuses on the development of teaching strategies in home economics such as self-sufficiency and self reliance through active learning. Visits to markets and restaurants foster better understanding of multicultural food customs.

results.title: Life and study at the university
results.brief_description: Ilustrates aspects of university life: getting a degree, studying, campus club, religious organizations, political groups, learning to think and be analytical and making friends. It concludes showing a number of famous students who choose careers completely different from those for which they studied.

results.title: Clickers club: photo adventures in curriculum
results.creator_credit: Jeff Spencer, Tony Paice and Corporate Development, Western Region and TV ED Productions
results.brief_description: Outlines the aims of a new photography club established throughout Victorian schools. Marg Sheehan interviews Leigh Hepburn who introduces the Project Kit, Roxanne Rodgers who explains its uses in an integrated curriculum and Gail Evans who applies the resources during a primary school class excursion to the zoo.

results.title: Remembering Janefield ...
results.creator_credit: Eastern Energy and Land Use Team, State of Victoria, Department of Human Services and Tribal Communications
results.brief_description: Provides a brief history of the Janefield Training Centre, Bundoora with both ex-residents and ex-staff members sharing anecdotes and moving reflections on their experiences and years at the institution.
